title: Greetings & introductions
canDo: I can greet someone, introduce myself, and ask their name.

chunks.example: Hi, I'm Sara. Nice to meet you.
Nice to meet you too!
Hi! How are you?
I'm fine, thanks. And you?
Hello! What's your name?

reading.passage: “Hi, I'm Sara. What's your name?” “I'm Omar. Nice to meet you!” “How are you, Omar?” “I'm fine, thanks. And you?”

vocabInContext.q: “Nice to meet you” is used when you…

script.t: Hi, I'm Sara. What's your name?
I'm Omar. Nice to meet you!

gist.q: Are they meeting for the first time?

fluency432.prompt: Introduce yourself: your name and how you are.

retell.prompt: Act out meeting Sara and Omar.

task.prompt: Meet a new person: greet them, say your name, and ask theirs.

writing.model: Hi! I'm Lena. Nice to meet you. How are you?

sentenceCombining.answer: Hi, I'm Sara — nice to meet you.
How are you? I'm fine, thanks.

task.prompt: Write a short hello message introducing yourself.

quiz.q: You meet someone new and want to know their name.

title: Personal information
canDo: I can say where I'm from, where I live, and my age.

chunks.example: Hi! Where are you from?
I'm from Canada, near Toronto.
I live in Toronto with my family.
I'm twenty years old.
I speak a little English and some French.

grammar.pattern: I live · you live · I speak · you speak

reading.passage: “Where are you from?” “I'm from Canada, but I live in Toronto. I'm twenty years old. I speak a little English and some French.”

questions.q: How many languages does she mention?

script.t: I'm from Canada. I live in Toronto.

fluency432.prompt: Say where you're from, where you live, and your age.

retell.prompt: Tell me about the person from Canada.

task.prompt: Ask someone about their background, and answer about yours.

writing.model: I'm from Canada. I live in Toronto. I'm twenty years old and I speak a little English.

sentenceCombining.answer: I'm from Canada, and I live in Toronto.
I'm twenty years old and I speak English.

task.prompt: Write 2–3 sentences about where you're from and where you live.

quiz.q: You want to know someone's country.

title: Family
canDo: I can talk about my family members.

chunks.example: This is my brother, Ali.

chunks.use: Say who is in your family.
chunks.example: I have two sisters and one brother.
I'm married with one child.
I have one child, a little boy.
We're a close family and see each other often.

reading.passage: This is my family. I have two sisters and one brother. I'm married and I have one child. We're a close family and we eat together every weekend.

questions.q: How many sisters does the writer have?

script.t: Do you have any brothers or sisters?

task.prompt: Tell a friend about your family.

writing.model: This is my family. I have two sisters and one brother. We're a close family.

sentenceCombining.answer: I have two sisters and one brother.
I'm married and I have one child.

quiz.q: You want to introduce your brother.

title: Daily routine
canDo: I can describe my daily routine and the times I do things.

chunks.example: On weekdays I get up at seven.

chunks.text: I go to work by bus.
chunks.example: I go to work by bus every day.
I have lunch at noon with colleagues.
What time do you start work?
I usually finish at five and go home.

reading.passage: On weekdays I get up at seven. I go to work by bus. I have lunch at noon and I usually finish at five. In the evening I relax at home.

questions.q: How does the writer go to work?

script.t: What time do you start work?
I start at nine and finish at five.

writing.model: I get up at seven. I go to work by bus. I usually finish at five.

sentenceCombining.answer: I get up at seven and go to work.
I have lunch at noon and finish at five.

task.prompt: Describe your morning routine in 2–3 sentences.

quiz.q: You want to ask when someone's workday begins.
Tell someone how you travel to work.
options: I go to work by bus.

title: Food & ordering
canDo: I can order food and drink and ask for the bill.

chunks.example: I'd like a coffee, please.
Hello, can I see the menu?
How much is it altogether?
And a glass of water, please.
Excuse me, can I have the bill?

grammar.focus: Polite requests: I'd like / Can I

reading.passage: At the café I say, “Can I see the menu?” Then, “I'd like a coffee, please, and a glass of water.” At the end I ask, “Can I have the bill?”

questions.q: What does the writer ask for at the end?

script.t: Yes, I'd like a coffee, please.

fluency432.prompt: Order a drink and a snack at a café.

task.prompt: Order at a café: ask for the menu, order, and ask for the bill.

writing.model: Can I see the menu? I'd like a coffee, please. Can I have the bill?

sentenceCombining.answer: I'd like a coffee and some water, please.
We've finished, so can I have the bill?

task.prompt: Write what you say to order a meal (2–3 sentences).

quiz.q: You want to order a drink politely.
You've finished and want to pay.

title: Shopping
canDo: I can ask prices, ask for sizes, and pay.

chunks.example: Excuse me, how much is this?
Can I pay by card?
I'm just looking, thanks.

chunks.text: Do you have a bigger size?
chunks.example: Do you have a bigger size?
It's perfect — I'll take it.

grammar.pattern: How much is this? · Can I…?

reading.passage: In the shop the assistant asks if I need help. “I'm just looking, thanks.” Later I ask, “How much is this? Do you have a bigger size?” Then, “I'll take it. Can I pay by card?”

questions.q: What does the customer say first?

gist.q: Does the customer want help now?

task.prompt: Buy an item: ask the price, ask for a size, and pay.

writing.model: How much is this? Do you have a bigger size? I'll take it — can I pay by card?

sentenceCombining.answer: I like this bag, so I'll take it.
How much is this, and can I pay by card?

task.prompt: Write a short shopping conversation (2–3 sentences).

quiz.q: You want to know an item's price.
A shop assistant offers help but you're browsing.
